title: Apply power reduction formulas for $$\sin**2(x)$$ and $$\cos**2(x)$$.
Expand the expression and identify remaining powers.
text: After substituting the reduction formulas, expand the product. You will likely have a $$\cos**2(2x)$$ term and a $$\cos**3(2x)$$ term that need further reduction.

title: Reduce $$\cos**3(u)$$ using a known formula.
text: Recall $$\cos(3x)$$ $$=$$ 4\cos**3(x) $$-$$ $$3\cos(x).$$ Alternatively, write $$\cos**3(u)$$ as $$\cos(u)\cos**2(u)$$. $$Apply$$ $$the$$ $$reduction$$ $$formula$$ $$for$$ $$\cos**2(u)$$ and then use the product-to-sum formula for $$\cos(A)\cos(B)$$, $$which$$ $$is$$ $$\frac{1}{2}(\cos(A-B)$$ + \cos(A+B))$$.
